body.title: Inference models registry: versioned entries with creation dates
body.result: created_at on ModelSpec, update_model() archives facia+retired_at, list_model_history() oldest-first, 8 tests; fb7ec20f
body.description:
As models are revved (e.g. deepseek-v3 → deepseek-v4, qwen3-coder-30b gets a patch), the old capability data should be preserved rather than overwritten. Pattern: the facia entry (identified by stable ID) gets updated with new data; the old entry is copied to a versioned archive row with a creation_date in metadata. This gives us a historical record of model capability evolution and allows rollback comparisons. The facia row retains the same DB row ID so existing rules-engine references stay valid.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/models_registry.py, devices/inference/sources.py, schema migration if models are stored in Postgres
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; versioned memory pattern (facia row stable, archive rows appended); creation_date in metadata
**Scope boundary:** In scope: update_model() method that archives old entry + updates facia; creation_date field in model metadata; read path unchanged. Out of scope: automated version detection (T-inference-models-autoversion), UI for browsing history.
**Completion criteria:** (1) update_model(id, new_data) archives the current entry with creation_date and updates the facia row; (2) list_model_history(id) returns archived versions in chronological order; (3) facia row ID is stable across updates; (4) tests: update preserves old data in archive; history returns versions in order; facia ID unchanged
